what_it_does: Records the outcome and isolated verification of the marks lane of an MCP-sweep, which built mcp_face/tools/marks.py — a single op-selector 'marks' tool consolidating three former flat tools (marks_for, marks_by_type, findings_for). Reports the module/tool, its ops, flat tools to remove vs keep, verification, status, and design notes.
observations: module: mcp_face/tools/marks.py; tool: 'marks' with three ops selected by a 'by' verb: target, type, findings.

by='target' wraps Suite.marks_for; by='type' wraps the REGISTRY-GATED Suite.marks_by_type (fail-loud on unknown type); by='findings' reaches store.findings_for for the distinct legacy coherence findings store.
flat_tools_to_remove: marks_for, marks_by_type, findings_for; flat_tools_to_keep: mark (the write tool, CQRS preserved); new_suite_methods: [].
Verified in isolation (not the whole server, since sibling lanes were mid-write): a throwaway FastMCP plus a real Suite, confirming the tool registers and round-trips on a live mark_type.
Gate test: marks(by='type', mark_type='not_a_real_type_xyz') raises ValueError listing registered types, because it wraps Suite.marks_by_type not store.marks_by_type.
Live mark_types observed from the registry (ai_fingerprint, built_twice, contradiction, gold_likelihood, overlap) — enums derive from the live registry, nothing hardcoded.

Documents a store asymmetry carried into the docstring: by='target'/by='type' read the marks store via Suite; by='findings' reads the distinct findings store via store, since there is no Suite.findings_for.
Flags (not built) a future Suite.findings_for wrapper so by='findings' could also go through the Suite, recommending the SUITE lane add it later (it would edit out-of-lane runtime/suite.py).
Per-branch concise projection because marks and findings have different schemas, deviating from corpus.py's single _shape.
Self-classifies status as built-verified; file-disjoint respected (only marks.py), not committed.
standouts: CQRS held: the write tool 'mark' is kept while the three flat read tools are folded into one op-selector.
The registry-gated by='type' path (wrapping the Suite, not the store) so unknown mark types fail loud.
The documented marks-store vs findings-store asymmetry, with a flagged-not-built Suite.findings_for seam.
conventions: Follows a per-lane report schema (module/tool/ops/flat_tools_*) rather than the role-lane schema.
Mirrors mcp_face/tools/corpus.py's op-selector + concise-default + teaching-errors pattern, with a per-branch concise projection.
Registry-is-truth on mark_type; the 'by' verb vocabulary is a hardcoded tuple like corpus.py's OPS.
File-disjoint respected, not committed; flat-tool removal deferred to the serial server.py pass.
notes: A build-lane status record: the marks sweep built a consolidated op-selector 'marks' read tool over the marks and findings stores, kept the 'mark' write tool, and flagged a future Suite.findings_for wrapper.
questions: When will the serial server.py pass actually remove the three flat tools, and is anything still importing them in the interim?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — a build report recording the consolidated tool built and verified in isolation.
apparent_themes: Consolidate flat tools into one op-selector
CQRS (separate the write tool from the read tool)
Registry-is-truth and fail-loud gating; carry asymmetry into the docstring
intention_signals: Aims to fold three mark/finding read tools into one teaching op-selector while preserving the registry gate and the two distinct stores, and to flag the longer-term Suite.findings_for symmetry without overstepping the lane.
novelty: The per-branch concise projection (two schemas under one tool) and the explicit marks-store vs findings-store asymmetry with a flagged Suite-wrapper seam.
contribution: Contributes the consolidated 'marks' tool design, its registry-gated type path, the CQRS keep-mark decision, and the documented store asymmetry plus the future Suite.findings_for seam, which a fused system would keep as the provenance of mark/finding read access.
summary_for_embedding: A seam-lane JSON report documenting the marks MCP-sweep lane's consolidated op-selector 'marks' read tool over the marks and findings stores, with a kept 'mark' write tool and a registry-gated type path.
intention_for_embedding: To consolidate the flat mark/finding read tools into one teaching op-selector while preserving CQRS, the registry gate, and the two distinct stores.
